prediction:
FROM customers c
| JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'paid'
| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG c.customer_id, c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS paid_order_count
repair_actions: Moved the order status filter from WHERE into the JOIN ON clause: added AND o.status = 'paid' to the JOIN condition.
Removed the WHERE o.status = 'paid' filter to avoid filtering out joined rows after the join.

prediction:
FROM products p
| JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
| JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
| WHERE o.status = 'paid'
| WHERE p.category = 'furniture'
| AGG COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS paid_orders_count
repair_actions: Replaced aggregation SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity with COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS paid_orders_count to return the number of paid orders for furniture rather than total item quantity (critic suggested possible mismatch between intended measure and aggregation).
